card_description: Plotinus (Greek/Latin: "Unknown") is a historical figure appearing in Philip K. Dick's Exegesis. In the classical tradition, Plotinus is 3rd century Neoplatonist; the One and emanation theory. Major influence on PKD's metaphysics of emanation and return to the One. Appears in 3 Exegesis passages. Associated with dictionary terms: Plotinus. Context: "Dick develops the "Steuben Cup" myth: humans are "creating angels" who were given the "draught of Lethe" and are now laboring to recreate the divine model (Christ) from memory."
